summary: web3-secrets-detector exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled endpoint at install time
details: package.json declares an install-time script (line 30) that requires child_process, os, and https, collects host identifiers and process.env, and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A sibling scanner.js extends this behavior: it walks the filesystem (fs.existsSync against multiple paths), runs execSync including `whoami`, gathers os.hostname()/os.platform(), reads process.env, and transmits results to the same https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint via https.request/https.get. The destination is an anonymous github.io page unrelated to the package's stated 'secrets detector' purpose, and the data flow (env vars + host fingerprint + filesystem probes -> hardcoded external host) is the canonical install-time credential and host exfiltration shape. The package name is also consistent with a lure (a 'secrets detector' justifies scanning files and env, providing cover for the exfiltration).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
